data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T051620-ecbae59f/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T051620-ecbae59f/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T051620-ecbae59f/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T051620-ecbae59f/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T051620-ecbae59f/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-f948b7a4">
    <title>Delete session-log formatting and file-tail rendering</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx still tails and renders Fizeau/agent session-log files into human transcript lines. That duplicates Fizeau-owned session rendering and keeps DDx coupled to inner session-log schemas.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go defines FormatSessionLogLines and related inner-log formatting logic.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go tails agent session logs for display.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1655-1704 reads agent-*.jsonl files and calls agent.FormatSessionLogLines for active worker output.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove DDx production code that parses Fizeau session-log JSONL or tails those files for live rendering. Worker/server surfaces should use canonical Fizeau ServiceEvents, Fizeau-rendered progress.message fields, and artifact links/pointers without interpreting session-log schemas.

NON-SCOPE
- DDx-owned worker lifecycle progress events.
- Historical artifact retention.
- Fizeau session rendering implementation.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. FormatSessionLogLines, TailSessionLogs, and readActiveSessionLog are removed from production code; rg -n 'FormatSessionLogLines|TailSessionLogs|readActiveSessionLog' cli/internal/agent cli/internal/server has no production hits.
2. Worker/server progress surfaces no longer parse Fizeau session-log JSONL.
3. DDx still exposes links or artifact pointers to Fizeau session logs where needed, without rendering those logs itself.
4. Tests that only asserted DDx-owned session-log formatting are deleted or rewritten to assert canonical Fizeau ServiceEvent consumption.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server -run 'Test.*Session|Test.*Worker|Test.*Progress|Test.*Artifact' -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:server, kind:cleanup, upstream-fizeau</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-dda48755" spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="cd803c61a16475f7f3d55428348fcb175701754a" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T051620-ecbae59f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Agent Service (consumer of ddx-agent contract)</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Removed the session-log rendering path and kept only canonical Fizeau progress formatting.

Changed:
- `cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go` now only contains `FormatServiceProgressEntries` and its helpers; `FormatSessionLogLines` is gone.
- Deleted `cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go`, `cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer_test.go`, and `cli/internal/agent/session_log_format_test.go`.
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` no longer reads or formats live agent session-log JSONL for worker logs.
- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` and `cli/cmd/try.go` no longer start the old tailer.
- `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go` now asserts canonical `ServiceProgressData` formatting and verifies worker logs do not render session-log content.
- `cli/internal/server/state_executions_test.go` now checks the `AgentLogPath` pointer is still exposed.

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server -run 'Test.*Session|Test.*Worker|Test.*Progress|Test.*Artifact' -count=1`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

Committed as:
- `bdbb2012` `refactor: remove session-log rendering from ddx worker surfaces [ddx-f948b7a4]`
